description: A round, deep dish used for food or liquids; a vessel that curves to hold. 'Noru' names what receives—the cupped shape that holds nourishment.
sound_symbolism: The word nestles inward. 'No' curves in gentle enclosure, space forming. Then 'ru' rounds the shape, bowl ready to receive.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'lo noru' (the bowl). Related to 'tomi' (pot) but open-topped for serving and eating.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Bowls held life—rice, soup, offerings, the humble vessel of daily sustenance.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: The receiving mind—like a bowl, open to what comes, holding without grasping.
